message:
chore: sync beads issue tracker with EXPIREAT event fix and conformance task

- Add frankenredis-g69j (closed): EXPIREAT now emits NOTIFY_EXPIRED with
  'expired' event instead of NOTIFY_GENERIC with 'del' when timestamp is
  in the past
- Add frankenredis-vfgb (in_progress): EXPIREAT/PEXPIREAT invalid integer
  conformance cases

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenredis-fw60","title":"Update FEATURE_PARITY.md Current State section to match code reality","description":"# FEATURE_PARITY Accuracy Fix

## What
Review every row in the parity matrix and verify the status against actual code.

## Known Issues
1. Replication: claimed parity_green, actually in_progress (separate bead handles this)
2. "Benchmark + optimization artifacts": claimed parity_green but artifacts measure conformance speed not workload performance — should note this

## Implementation
1. Verify each parity_green claim has backing evidence (tests, code, e2e)
2. Add notes column clarifying what "green" means for each row
3. Add "Last Verified" date column
4. If any other inflated claims are found, downgrade them

## Depends On
- Replication status downgrade bead (for that specific row)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:45.557114056Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T19:11:13.530402749Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T19:11:13.530131655Z","close_reason":"Reviewed all parity matrix rows. Downgraded 'Benchmark + optimization artifacts' from parity_green to in_progress (conformance optimization artifacts exist but workload benchmarks are missing). Replication was already downgraded in frankenredis-8xmq. Other rows verified as accurate.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-fw60","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-8xmq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:20.773483509Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenredis-g2wa","title":"Implement Lua coroutine stubs that return proper Redis-compatible errors","description":"# Lua Coroutine Stubs

## What
The hand-written Lua interpreter (4,750 lines) correctly implements most of Lua 5.1 but is missing coroutines (create/resume/yield/status). While rarely used in Redis Lua scripts, calling them currently produces confusing errors.

## Implementation
Add stub functions that return clear error messages:
- coroutine.create → error "coroutines are not supported in FrankenRedis Lua"
- coroutine.resume → same
- coroutine.yield → same
- coroutine.status → same

This matches Redis behavior (Redis also does not support coroutines in EVAL).

## Files
- crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs (add coroutine table stubs)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:13:08.225720832Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:05:14.389579771Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:05:14.389269604Z","close_reason":"Added coroutine table with stubs for create/resume/yield/status/wrap/running. All return 'attempt to call a nil value' error, matching Redis behavior. Added 2 tests: coroutine_stubs_return_error and coroutine_table_is_accessible.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -289,6 +290,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenredis-v7zc","title":"Add pub/sub cross-client integration test via TCP","description":"# Pub/Sub Cross-Client TCP Test

## What
An integration test that demonstrates pub/sub message delivery between multiple TCP clients connected to FrankenRedis.

## Why
Pub/sub delivery is tested via internal APIs (conformance fixtures) but never through actual independent TCP connections. A subscriber on one connection must receive messages published from a completely separate connection.

## Implementation

### Test 1: Basic Pub/Sub
1. Start frankenredis on random port
2. Client A (thread 1): SUBSCRIBE channel1, wait for subscription confirmation
3. Client B (thread 2): PUBLISH channel1 "hello" → verify returns 1 (one subscriber)
4. Client A: Read next message, verify ["message", "channel1", "hello"]
5. Client A: UNSUBSCRIBE channel1

### Test 2: Pattern Subscribe
1. Client A: PSUBSCRIBE "news.*"
2. Client B: PUBLISH news.sports "goal!" → returns 1
3. Client A: Verify ["pmessage", "news.*", "news.sports", "goal!"]

### Test 3: Multiple Subscribers
1. Client A: SUBSCRIBE channel1
2. Client C: SUBSCRIBE channel1
3. Client B: PUBLISH channel1 "broadcast" → returns 2
4. Both A and C receive the message

### Test 4: Unsubscribe stops delivery
1. Client A: SUBSCRIBE channel1
2. Client B: PUBLISH channel1 "msg1" → A receives
3. Client A: UNSUBSCRIBE channel1
4. Client B: PUBLISH channel1 "msg2" → returns 0 (no subscribers)

## Logging
- Log all pub/sub operations with timestamps
- Log message received events: "[ClientA] RECEIVED: message channel1 hello"
- Log subscriber counts on PUBLISH

## Files
- crates/fr-server/tests/pubsub_e2e.rs (new)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:21.136076437Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T19:05:42.368395054Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T19:05:42.368142626Z","close_reason":"Added 3 pub/sub E2E tests to tcp_e2e_test.rs: tcp_pubsub_basic_cross_client_delivery (subscribe/publish/unsubscribe lifecycle), tcp_pubsub_multiple_subscribers (broadcast to 2 clients), tcp_pubsub_pattern_subscribe (PSUBSCRIBE with pmessage delivery). All pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
